author: Crg878
msg: Over the past three months before the reductions, I’ve been experimenting with different setups for maximising rewards. On Virgin Broadband I found that running around 8–10 phones seemed to be the sweet spot. At one point, I had three TP-Link routers, each using EE data-only SIMs, with about 8 phones connected per router. This setup averaged roughly $0.23 per phone per day, much the same as virgin. Initially, returns were much lower around $0.03 per phone, but they gradually increased over two months to about $0.23 just before the reductions. From my testing, 8 phones per router seemed to give the best results, just like virgin. I chose EE SIMs because they provide a strong signal in my area. However, even though the IP addresses are technically different for each sim, some parts of them appear the same. Based on the limited information available, I suspect the system may still recognise them as coming from the same source. This is why I think location could play a role. If others nearby are running similar setups, it might affect my results. I’m not sure if this relates to the “10–20 devices per network subnet” limit that’s been mentioned, but it seems possible. My next step is to diversify by using SIMs from other networks like Three and Vodafone, problem is signal not great.  I’ll make those changes and then leave everything running to see if performance stabilises over the next couple of weeks.

This recalibration process is making me angry. No network change takes this long. It's just a technical sounding word that explains nothing with no end date. I have emailed support and they cannot provide an end date. They will not provide support until recalibration has complete. 
The rewards were crap before but at least there was something. Has anyone got any insight into what the team are doing and when they will tell the UNOs?

author: Darren @WorldDePIN
msg: You should not have to guess mate. The team should provide transparency with how rewards are calculated. This recalibration has punished honest actors, which is unfair. The lack of communication and an end date reflects a complete lack of respect by the Unity team towards UNOs.

author: Kyle | Will never DM you first
msg:
The suggestion is a factory reset and updating to the latest android version possible for your device. 

The error means that there was an issue validating the integrity of the device. 

This could be caused by anything that modifies system-level services. Samsung is usually good at keeping their devices Integrity-compliant.

author: Rick Middleton
msg:
Yes... of course there is better quality of service from different ISPs.   The physical tower you connect to is the same.. but what happens on the path of your data after is different.

Some simple examples - in the US you get 'prority' data.. high speed..  depends how much you pay for your plan..   once it runs out you get lower speed.

WM plans have a built in VPN server side so your data is encrypted along the way

I've not looked into how ISPs share IP addresses between phone users.. but it is not hard to imagine how they would do it...  Let's go ask AI for fun

author: Rick Middleton
msg:
There we go..  I learned something by asking AI.

I now know what CGNAT is... and I also know that some home broadband lines use it now as well as mobile networks as IPV4 addresses run out / become scarce.

One thing it said that I had not seen mentioned is that many providers are swapping to IPV6 to give unique addresses.. but mostly cheaper providers are still using IPV4

Before I start I wanted to check.. does Unity plan to create an official AI support agent for troubleshooting (possibly as a mobile app or in-app tool) in the future? ie. Like Unity Asst in the UNO channel. Many ULO’s have asked questions I couldn’t answer accurately and searching through chats takes a lot of time. author: Kay | Will never DM you first
msg:
[Media] If you create a lease and apply custom settings to a license, those settings will remain after the lease is revoked. You can manually reset the license back to wallet defaults. Creating a custom lease itself does not change the license settings

So: the system does NOT automatically revert to your wallet default settings.

And I see your point how it can automatically mean lots of manual work after bulk revoking, so I suggest you fill out the form how you would like to see it work/add button/add settings/etc here: https://forms.gle/womUzD5u2fbEGbbHA

author: Krabalot
msg:
I haven't received a single CLI nor SMS task since their launch. 

A task pays upon completion of said task. If you don't get assigned a task, you will not be able to complete it. Thus, you won't be paid.

If someone received and completed the task AND was NOT paid, then you would be correct in saying that the tasks don't pay. 

The ONLY truth we can seek is the numerical volume that is currently present on the network.

author: J. P. Costa
msg:
Whatever man, I think my question was clear enough dont you?
The team loves playing with words, so we get “released” tasks that have zero volume (ie no payments)
All we want is a task that starts to pay regularly from the moment its live, unlike what happened with cli/sms, is that better now?

author: Peter
msg:
That’s true. Many early investors put in significant time and effort—purchasing nodes and equipment, recruiting users, and spending countless hours experimenting with different setups. It’s important to highlight that much of this was done in the absence of clear guidance or consistent information from the team. Attempts to communicate and get reliable answers were often inconsistent.

These early participants were essentially pioneers, trying to make the system work both for themselves and for the success of the project. Unfortunately, many have since stepped away after repeatedly running into dead ends without clear explanations.

author: Jouni Kuisma
msg:
#amaquestions 
Does the Unity team know how worried some of the UNOs are right now? Some of the UNOs are losing ULOs in fast pace as the rewards are so poor and the current ROI for our investment is quite horrible for most. With current levels the ULOs will keep diminishing. 

So when is it expected for both ULOs and UNOs to earn decent rewards? After all, your ”conservative” estimate was $96 per license/month. How about even a quarter of that? I understand it is difficult to give an exact date but will it happen in a couple of weeks or months or maybe next year? TY 🙏

author: Villion
msg: For anyone interested in the U.S., I just got 10 eSIMs active on my phones on Friday. Unlimited talk, text, data. $10 a month through Verizon on a business promo that ends on the 14th, so I was told. You are also required to port the lines in but Number Barn helps hurdle that requirement.

author: Villion
msg: …this is for the ULO phones I have at home of course. Not promoting Verizon they just have the best coverage in my area, which is a plus for completing tasks. Would love to switch these to WM lines whenever we get the green light.
